creative_brief: Clean, editorial layout. No hero image needed. Use a single pull-quote callout: 'Values and money, finally pointing the same direction.' Minimal colour, high contrast. The copy does the work.
subject: Supermarkets don't fail farmers by accident
preheader: It's structural. And there's one way to build something that works differently.
body:
Hi {first_name},

Most people assume the big chains squeeze farmers because they're greedy.

It's not greed. It's structure. When shareholders own the supply chain, cutting the farmer's margin is just good business.

That's not a bug. That's the model.

So here's the question we kept coming back to when we built FarmThru: what if the people who care about the food own the store?

Not a charity. Not a co-op that charges to join. A proper equity stake, with a low minimum entry.

Here's how it works.

FarmThru is raising through Birchal, Australia's leading equity crowdfunding platform. The round opens 23 June 2026.

We have 15 to 20 partner farms already supplying the Brookvale hub. Farms like Bundarra (pork), Collins Wild Caught (South Coast seafood), Farmer Brown (eggs), Paris Creek Farms (bio-dynamic dairy from Meadows, SA).

No warehouses. No wholesalers. Straight from farm to hub.

When you invest, you're not donating to a cause. You're owning a piece of the supply chain that pays farmers first.

Values and money, finally pointing the same direction.

As a non-VIP waitlist member, you'll get access when the round opens publicly on 23 June. Upgrade to VIP with a small refundable deposit and you'll see the offer 24 hours before everyone else.

No commitment to invest now. Just a decision about whether you want to be part of building this.

Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
user_review_note: We need to remove anything about investing dollar amounts. No minimum investment, no maximum. We can say that we are going to do a raise, but not those parts.
